metadata.title:
WHAT THE COUNCIL


DID LAST NIGHT

pages.raw_text:
four specials.


The bill went through the second
reading and will probably come up
at the next meeting.


Boss Pratt and his followers will
fight the bill in an effort to secure
the naming of the new police cap
tain. Detective D. d. McPhee is .
candidate for the Office and has the
backing Of the administration forces.
Sergeant John Sullivan is the only
other candidate. He has the back
ing of the moral element of the COM
mittee.


Through the pledges made to Pratt
for his work in the board of eQuahza-
tion the Spokesman-Review was last
night compelled to swing into line
for Sergeant Sullivan after fighting
that officer for the past two years.


The Washington Water Power COM
pany filed an acceptance of the Hang
man creek bridge franchise and fled
bond for $1,000 for the faithful
performance of its contract with the
city.


The offer of Chester Glass to donate
site for the Lidgerwood standpipe
was referred to the board of public
works.


DIR. Genoway of the board of health
fled a report that the cost of oper-
ating the new crematory is 80 cents
a ton. The stipulation was that the
crematory would be operated at q
oaat nf SO captc ton


The health Office syndicate succeed
ed in rushing through the appoint
ment of e. w. Otis as mounted sani-
tary policeman in place of j. w.
Thayer A strong fight was made
against the appointment on the
ground that Otis had been disgraced
while superintendent of the isolation
hospital.


Interesting developments are prom
Used over the ousting Of Thayer and
the appointment Of Otis. which 10
claimed to have been done In order
to forward a graft by the board Of
health syndicate seeking to control
the scavenger business
